text: Communication history in socialist Europe is associated largely with propaganda. The USSR became the world’s first propaganda state in the sense of Peter Kenez’s expression describing a radically new system of communication which was later copied by other countries in Eastern Europe. Kenez published his classic study on The Birth of the Propaganda Machine. Studies in the Technique of Bolshevik Conquest (1985), in which he described how the Bolsheviks were different, and in which they tried to create a ‘new man’ in order to establish a new society using ‘political education’. Propaganda in the Soviet case was a mechanism to carry out this aim in societal transformation.
headline: Fully Human Written
